channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Tiger's son makes the ace. Bernhard Langer makes the winning putt in playoff over Woods at PNC
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Tiger Woods' son hit the shot of his life for his first hole-in-one. Bernhard Langer hit the shot that mattered Sunday, an 18-foot eagle putt in a playoff to beat Woods and son Charlie in a terrific duel at the PNC Championship.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas AG sues NCAA over trans inclusion in women's sports
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton accuses the NCAA of violating the Texas Trade Practices Act for allowing transgender athletes to compete in women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 49ers eliminated from NFC playoffs before game against Miami. Cowboys, Saints, Cardinals also out
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — The defending NFC champion San Francisco 49ers were eliminated from the playoffs before their game at Miami began on Sunday because of victories by Washington and the Los Angeles Rams.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rangers forward Matt Rempe suspended for eight games for elbow on Heiskanen
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York Rangers forward Matt Rempe was suspended for eight games by NHL’s Department of Player Safety on Sunday for boarding and elbowing Dallas Stars defenseman Miro Heiskanen on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: ALLAN KREDA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jared Goff throws 3 TD passes as Lions beat Bears 34-17 for franchise-record 13th win
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Jared Goff threw for 336 yards and three touchdowns, and the Detroit Lions remained on top of the NFC with their franchise-record 13th win, 34-17 over the Chicago Bears on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Boise State's Jeanty is the best back in the nation. Penn State's duo could be right behind him
item.description: Boise State's Ashton Jeanty is the best running back in the country, with the stats and the hardware to prove it. Jeanty's ever-churning legs are the biggest — but not only — reasons why the Broncos stormed to a 12-1 record and the third seed in the College Football Playoff.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eagles’ Jalen Hurts ruled out with a concussion in first quarter vs Commanders
item.description: Philadelphia Eagles quarterback Jalen Hurts was ruled out of Sunday's game against the Washington Commanders after sustaining a concussion in the first quarter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas' goal-line wall will be tested by Arizona State's wrecking ball running back Scattebo
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — The Texas defense seems to have built a big burnt orange wall at the goal line. Late-game goal-line stands have helped keep the Longhorns moving toward a possible national championship.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Texans WR Tank Dell released from hospital, headed back to Houston after severe knee injury
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Texans receiver Tank Dell has been released after an overnight stay in the hospital and was traveling back to Houston on Sunday after sustaining a severe knee injury in Saturday’s loss to the Kansas City Chiefs.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIE RIEKEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lindsey Vonn took all of 20 seconds to regain her former speed in comeback race at age 40
item.description: ST. MORITZ, Switzerland (AP) — Maybe it was nerves for her first World Cup race back in nearly six years. Maybe it was a bumpier course than she's used to racing on. Maybe it was some wrong lines or angles.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DAMPF, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bears vs. Lions: Injuries, inactives and key matchups for Sunday's game at Soldier Field
item.description: UPDATE: The Bears and Lions have announced their inactives for Sunday's game. See INJURY REPORT below. CHICAGO — The Bears are "playing out the string," as they say, eliminated from NFL playoff contention with three games still left to play this season.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Tiger Woods and son Charlie share the lead at PNC Championship in Orlando
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Tiger Woods and 15-year-old son Charlie ran off five straight birdies on the back nine Saturday for a 13-under 59 in the scramble format, giving them a share of the lead in the PNC Championship in Woods' first competition since back surgery in September.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Texas beats Clemson 38-24, advances to face Arizona State in College Football Playoff quarters
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Jaydon Blue had the biggest run in the biggest game of the season so far for Texas. He had to fight through a nagging ankle injury and fumble problems to do it.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ravens take down Steelers to keep AFC North race open
item.description: The Baltimore Ravens punched their ticket to the postseason and kept their hopes for a division title alive with a win over the Pittsburgh Steelers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas holds off Clemson's comeback to advance in College Football Playoff
item.description: The Texas Longhorns took care of business against the Clemson Tigers Saturday to advance to the quarterfinals of the College Football Playoff.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jeremiyah Love overcomes injury and illness to propel Notre Dame into 1st Sugar Bowl since 2007
item.description: SOUTH BEND, Ind. (AP) — Notre Dame running back Jeremiyah Love wasted no time sending a message to his teammates Friday night. Nothing was going to derail the seventh-seeded Fighting Irish in the College Football Playoff.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texans WR Tank Dell hospitalized after severe knee injury on TD catch vs. Chiefs
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Texans wide receiver Tank Dell was taken to the University of Kansas Medical Center and the team said he would remain overnight after sustaining a severe left knee injury while making a 30-yard touchdown catch in a 27-19 loss to the Chiefs on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Northwestern demolishes DePaul in crosstown showdown
item.description: EVANSTON, Ill. (WGN) — An early blitz on the offensive end and playmaking down the stretch from Brooks Barnhizer and Nick Martinelli helped seal a convincing win for Northwestern over DePaul in the yearly crosstown showdown between the two programs.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Texans' Tank Dell sustains gruesome leg injury, leaving teammates in tears
item.description: Houston Texans wide receiver Tank Dell was transported in an ambulance to a hospital during Saturday’s game against the Kansas City Chiefs after sustaining a gruesome leg injury.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rickey Henderson, MLB's all-time stolen base leader, dies at 65
item.description: Rickey Henderson, the man who changed Major League Baseball with his blazing, unbeatable speed, has died, the league confirmed. The all-time MLB leader in stolen bases and one of the games greatest leadoff hitters died in Oakland on Friday after a bout with pneumonia, TMZ reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Falcons expected to release Kirk Cousins after just one season following lackluster play: report
item.description: The Atlanta Falcons are expected to release quarterback Kirk Cousins after just one season due to lackluster play, with rookie Michael Penix Jr. taking his place.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Marco Rubio's son scores first collegiate touchdown in Florida’s blowout victory over Tulane in bowl game
item.description: Florida running back Anthony Rubio, the son of U.S. Senator Marco Rubio, scored his first collegiate touchdown in Friday's victory over Tulane in the Gasparilla Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UCSD basketball heating up, Tritons on a tear after historic upset
item.description: Fresh after pulling off what might be one of the biggest wins in program history, UCSD men's basketball has no plans on slowing down.
item.dc:creator: Bri Savant, Fox 5

item.title: Guenther scores twice as Utah HC beats Minnesota, 2-1
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club is in seventh heaven right about now. Dylan Guenther scored twice, including the game-winner at 10:01 of the third period, as Utah ran its road winning streak to seven in a row Friday night with a hard-fought 2-1 victory over the Minnesota Wild.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Utes land talented running back Wayshawn Parker
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utes football team may have picked up its biggest off-season acquisition yet. Washington State running back Wayshawn Parker, considered one of the top backs in the transfer portal, announced he will play for the Utes next season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Broncos show growing pains in second-half collapse to Chargers
item.description: In a pivotal Thursday Night, AFC West battle in Los Angeles with plenty of playoff implications, we saw no lack of offense.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Bay FC Treasure Island training facility lease approved by SF mayor
item.description: (BCN) --San Francisco Mayor London Breed signed legislation on Thursday that will allow the women's professional soccer team Bay Football Club, or Bay FC, to make Treasure Island their future home with a permanent training center.
item.dc:creator: Bay City News, KRON4

item.title: Bucs' OC Liam Coen addresses NFL head coaching rumors
item.description: Tampa Bay Buccaneers' first-year offensive coordinator Liam Coen has been excellent calling plays for the Bucs this season.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trevor Baskin: From Division II star to Division I grinder
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — From Division II standout to Division I contributor, Trevor Baskin’s basketball journey has been anything but ordinary. After excelling at Colorado Mesa University, Baskin now competes at the highest level of college basketball with the Colorado Buffaloes.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: NCAA president says there are 'less than 10' transgender athletes in college sports
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker said it is “debatable” whether transgender women will “always” have a physical advantage over cisgender, or non-transgender, women.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: American competitive eater Joey Chestnut named Holiday Bowl Parade Grand Marshall
item.description: World-famous competitive eater Joey Chestnut has been chosen as the 2024 DIRECTV Holiday Bowl "Chief Eggnog Official." He will also serve as the Grand Marshal of the Holiday Bowl Parade.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: College hockey player sued by opponent over injury she suffered on the ice
item.description: The lawsuit claims an illegal hit from Crawford during a game on Feb. 19, 2023, has caused her serious long-term issues.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, NewsNation

item.title: NCAA president says there are 'less than 10' transgender athletes in college sports
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker told a Senate panel that there are fewer than 10 transgender athletes he is aware of who currently compete in college sports, pouring cold water on an issue Republicans have said is a nationwide problem and one that is increasingly fraught territory for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5
